payload.instructions:
# Repository Guidelines

## Project Structure & Module Organization
- Rails app with business logic in `app/controllers`, `app/models`, `app/services`, `app/workers`, and `app/views`; background jobs via Sidekiq.
- Frontend lives in `app/frontend` (Webpacker): entry packs in `app/frontend/packs`, shared components in `app/frontend/components`, namespaces for feature slices, and supporting libs in `app/frontend/lib`.
- Tests: Ruby specs in `spec` (models, requests, services, etc.); JS/React tests run from `app/frontend` and `jest` (mocks/setup) with coverage output to `coverage/`.
- Configuration in `config/` (environment files, initializers, routes); database schema and migrations in `db/`; static assets and compiled outputs in `public/`.

## Setup, Build, and Run
- First time: `bin/setup` (installs gems, yarn packages, prepares database).
- Run app locally: `bundle exec rails s` (Web, http://anedot.me:3000), `bundle exec sidekiq` (jobs), `bin/webpack-dev-server` (hot reloading for frontend).
- Database utilities: `bundle exec rails db:migrate` to apply migrations; `bundle exec rails db:reset` to rebuild from scratch.
- API docs: `RAILS_ENV=test bundle exec rake rswag:specs:swaggerize` to regenerate Swagger; open with `yarn docs:open:mac` or `yarn docs:open:windows`.

## Coding Style & Naming Conventions
- Ruby: enforced by RuboCop (Rails + RSpec cops); 2-space indentation, ~100-char lines, snake_case file names, CamelCase classes/modules. Prefer service objects and POROs in `app/services` for new logic.
- JS/React: ESLint + Prettier defaults; keep shared UI in `app/frontend/components`, feature-specific code under `app/frontend/namespaces/*`. Name hooks `useSomething`, components `PascalCase`, and tests `*.spec.js`/`*.test.js` near the code or under `jest/`.
- Styles: follow existing SCSS/CSS modules and avoid introducing global selectors unless intentional.

## Testing Guidelines
- Backend: `bundle exec rspec`; keep fixtures/factories close to specs and favor request specs for API changes. Use `bundle exec rake database_consistency` when adjusting schemas.
- Frontend: `yarn jest` (collects coverage by default); rely on Testing Library helpers set up in `jest/__setup__`. Mock network calls with `axios-mock-adapter` where possible.
- New features should include happy-path and failure coverage; update swagger specs when API contracts change.

## Commit & Pull Request Guidelines
- Commits: concise, imperative messages per Tim Pope’s guidance (e.g., “Fix donor validation”), grouped logically.
- PR titles: prefix with a tag such as `[BUGFIX]`, `[FEATURE]`, `[INTERNAL]`, `[MAINTENANCE]`, or `[SECURITY]` followed by a short summary.
- PR body: fill the template (summary, linked issues, screenshots for UI, test/QA checkboxes, cross-browser notes). Label appropriately (e.g., `needs review(s)`, `needs QA`) and request at least one approval. Rebase onto latest `master` before merging to avoid merge bubbles.

**Providing temporary CSRF bypass options**

Suggest setting an env var (e.g., `DISABLE_CSRF_FOR_LOCAL=1`) and adding a conditional skip in ApplicationController to disable CSRF verification in development when the var is set. Alternatively, recommend skipping CSRF verification or using `protect_from_forgery with: :null_session` in specific API controllers for local testing, with a strong caution to re-enable protections afterward and avoid committing these changes without environment guards.

payload.message:
You can turn off CSRF/session requirements just for local development and turn them back on afterwards. Easiest approach:

- Add a dev-only guard to `app/controllers/application_controller.rb`:
  ```rb
  skip_before_action :verify_authenticity_token, if: -> { Rails.env.development? && ENV['DISABLE_CSRF_LOCAL'] == '1' }
  protect_from_forgery with: :null_session, if: -> { Rails.env.development? && ENV['DISABLE_CSRF_LOCAL'] == '1' }
  ```
  Then start your server with `DISABLE_CSRF_LOCAL=1 bundle exec rails s`. Postman can now call endpoints without fetching a CSRF token or sending the session cookie.

- When you’re done, unset the env var or remove the snippet to restore normal protection.

If you prefer a global switch without code changes, you can set in `config/environments/development.rb`:
```rb
config.action_controller.allow_forgery_protection = false
```
then restart the server. Just remember to revert before committing.

# Public: Base class for Rails controllers
class ApplicationController < ActionController::Base
  include ControllerDeviseable
  include FeatureAccessible::DisbursementProfiles
  include FeatureAccessible::VendorReports
  include ParameterTraceable
  include CaseTransformable

  protect_from_forgery prepend: true

  before_action :apply_default_headers
  around_action :set_time_zone, if: :current_user

  def default_url_options
    Anedot::Application.routes.default_url_options
  end

private

  def apply_default_headers
    response.headers["X-Revision"] = Anedot::REVISION
  end

  def current_account_scope
    if current_user.anedot_staff?
      Account
    else
      current_user.accounts
    end
  end

  def set_time_zone(&)
    Time.use_zone(SupportedTimezones::DEFAULT_TZ_ID, &)
  end

  # Return a URI object for the referrer
  def parsed_referrer
    URI.parse(request.referer)
  rescue URI::InvalidURIError
    URI.parse("")
  end

  def find_impersonated_user
    if current_user.anedot_staff? && params[:user_id].present?
      User.find_by!(uid: params[:user_id])
    else
      current_user
    end
  end

  def writing_db_role(&)
    ActiveRecord::Base.connected_to(role: :writing, &)
  end
end

# frozen_string_literal: true

# base class for v3 api
class ApiApplicationController < ApplicationController
  rescue_from StandardError,
              ArgumentError,
              NotImplementedError, with: :internal_server_error
  rescue_from ActionController::ParameterMissing, with: :unprocessable_content
  rescue_from Pundit::NotAuthorizedError, with: :forbidden
  rescue_from ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound, with: :not_found

  include Pagination
  include ParamsWrapper
  include Idempotency

  before_action :clean_pagination_params
  before_action :transform_params_keys
  before_action :reject_html_requests

  protect_from_forgery

private

  def serialize_collection(collection, status: :ok, **opts)
    if opts.delete(:without_count)
      collection = collection.without_count
      opts[:meta] ||= {
        previousPage: collection.prev_page,
        currentPage: collection.current_page,
        nextPage: collection.next_page
      }
    else
      opts[:meta] ||= {
        currentPage: collection.current_page,
        totalPages: collection.total_pages,
        totalCount: collection.total_count
      }
    end

    opts[:root] = :data

    serialize(collection, status, opts)
  end

  def serialize_obj(obj, status: :ok, **opts)
    serialize(obj, status, opts)
  end

  def serialize(data, status, opts)
    serializer = serializer_class(opts[:serializer] || self.class)

    render json: serializer.render(data, opts),
           status:
  end

  def serialize_errors_for(obj, status: :unprocessable_content)
    render json: ErrorSerializer.render(obj), status:
  end

  def clean_pagination_params
    request.params.delete_if { |k, v| %w[page per_page].include?(k) && v.blank? }
  end

  def transform_params_keys
    request.params.deep_transform_keys! { |k| k.to_s.underscore }
  end

  def reject_html_requests
    head :not_acceptable if request.format.html?
  end

  def serializer_class(klass)
    return klass if klass.to_s.include?("Serializer")

    Object.const_get(klass.to_s.remove("Controller").singularize << "Serializer")
  end

  def internal_server_error(err)
    Anedot::ExceptionLogger.error(err)
    head :internal_server_error
  end

  def unprocessable_content(err)
    Anedot::ExceptionLogger.warn(err)
    head :unprocessable_content
  end

  def forbidden
    head :forbidden
  end

  def not_found
    head :not_found
  end
end

# Public: Controller concern for Devise
module ControllerDeviseable
  include ApplicationHelper
  include Helpers::FrontendXUrlHelper
  extend ActiveSupport::Concern

  included do
    before_action :clear_stored_location_from_other_domains
  end

private

  # Devise: override path redirected to after login
  def after_sign_in_path_for(resource_or_scope)
    if session["canny_sso_redirect"].present?
      url = "#{ENV["CANNY_REDIRECT_URL"]}?ssoToken=#{create_canny_token}"
      session.delete("canny_sso_redirect")
      return url
    elsif params["redirect"].present?
      return "#{ENV["CANNY_REDIRECT_URL"]}?ssoToken=#{create_canny_token}"
    end

    stored_location = stored_location_for(resource_or_scope)

    (stored_location != "/" && stored_location.presence) ||
      fallback_after_sign_in_path
  end

  def create_canny_token
    user_data = {
      email: current_user.email,
      id: current_user.uid,
      name: current_user.name
    }

    JWT.encode(user_data, ENV["CANNY_PRIVATE_SSO_KEY"], "HS256")
  end

  def fallback_after_sign_in_path
    # if the referrer is /donor/login or we have arrived here w/ no
    # referrer since the user is already logged in, redirect
    # to the donor experience dashboard
    if request.referer&.include?("donor/login") || request.path_info.include?("donor/login")
      user_dashboard_url(current_user)
    else
      accounts_list_url
    end
  end

  def user_provided_logout_url
    return unless request.params[:next]

    if params[:custom_domain] && CustomDomain.hostname_active?(params[:custom_domain])
      donor_logout_url(
        {
          host: request.params[:custom_domain],
          next: request.params[:next]
        }
      )
    else
      # https://github.com/heartcombo/devise/blob/5d5636f03ac19e8188d99c044d4b5e90124313af/lib/devise/controllers/store_location.rb#L56
      path = extract_path_from_location(request.params[:next])
      uri = Addressable::URI.join(request.base_url, path)
      uri.to_s
    end
  end

  def sign_out
    omniauth = session["devise.omniauth_data"]
    if omniauth && omniauth["provider"] == "account_oidc"
      account = Account.find_by!(uid: omniauth["uid"].partition("/").first)
      @logout_url = account.account_oidc_config.omniauth_options["logout_url"]
    end
    super
  end

  # Devise: override path redirected to after logout
  def after_sign_out_path_for(_resource_or_scope)
    user_provided_logout_url or @logout_url or login_path
  end

  # Only requests from the main Anedot application domain
  # should keep a stored location because it will cause a 404
  # if the user signs in and it redirects to a non-existent path on main domain.
  def clear_stored_location_from_other_domains
    return if AnedotDomainConstraint.matches?(request)

    store_location_for(:user, "")
  end
end

The codebase disables CSRF protection only in the test environment, not in development. Engineers test new endpoints by logging into the UI to get session cookies and CSRF tokens or by creating API tokens (e.g., Doorkeeper). Request specs typically use test helpers that bypass forgery protection, and webhook controllers explicitly skip CSRF checks. No built-in dev toggles exist to disable CSRF protection for local API testing.

payload.message:
No built-in “turn off CSRF in development” switch in this repo. `config/environments/test.rb` disables forgery protection for specs, but `development` keeps it on and `ApiApplicationController` still calls `protect_from_forgery`, so anything hitting Devise-backed endpoints needs a session + authenticity token. The only places that skip CSRF are specific webhooks/controllers (e.g., `callbacks/messages`, `api/public/v3/*_webhooks_controller.rb`).

What an engineer here typically does:
- Add request specs in `spec/requests/...` (or the relevant namespace) to exercise a new endpoint; test env already bypasses CSRF, and you can `sign_in` helpers as needed.
- For manual checks, log in via the web UI at `http://anedot.me:3000`, grab `_anedot_session` and the `X-CSRF-Token` from a page, and reuse them in Postman/curl.
- If you really need to bypass locally, you’d add a temporary dev-only guard (e.g., disable forgery protection in `config/environments/development.rb` or `skip_before_action :verify_authenticity_token` behind an env flag) and restart—just don’t commit that.

If you want, I can wire up a dev-only env flag to disable CSRF so you can flip it on/off without hunting for tokens.

# frozen_string_literal: true

require "base64"

module AccountDocuments
  # Uses Amazon Bedrock to validate that a document matches its claimed type.
  class BedrockValidator
    class ConfigurationError < StandardError; end
    class ValidationError < StandardError; end

    MAX_BASE64_LENGTH = 400_000
    DEFAULT_MODEL_ID = ENV.fetch("BEDROCK_MODEL_ID", "anthropic.claude-3-haiku-20240307")
    REGION = ENV.fetch("AWS_BEDROCK_REGION", "us-east-1")

    def initialize(document:, claimed_type: nil, client: nil, model_id: DEFAULT_MODEL_ID)
      @document = document
      @claimed_type = claimed_type || document.document_type
      @client = client || default_client
      @model_id = model_id
    end

    def call
      raise ConfigurationError, "Bedrock client is not configured" unless @client
      raise ValidationError, "Document file missing" unless @document.file&.file

      body = request_body.to_json
      response = @client.invoke_model(
        model_id: @model_id,
        body: body,
        content_type: "application/json",
        accept: "application/json"
      )

      parsed = parse_response(response)

      {
        verdict: parsed.fetch("verdict"),
        confidence: parsed.fetch("confidence"),
        reason: parsed.fetch("reason"),
        model_id: @model_id
      }
    rescue Aws::Errors::ServiceError => e
      raise ValidationError, "Bedrock request failed: #{e.message}"
    rescue JSON::ParserError => e
      raise ValidationError, "Unable to parse Bedrock response: #{e.message}"
    end

  private

    def default_client
      credentials = if ENV["AWS_BEDROCK_ACCESS_KEY_ID"] && ENV["AWS_BEDROCK_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY"]
                      Aws::Credentials.new(
                        ENV["AWS_BEDROCK_ACCESS_KEY_ID"],
                        ENV["AWS_BEDROCK_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY"]
                      )
                    end

      Aws::BedrockRuntime::Client.new(region: REGION, credentials:)
    rescue Aws::Errors::MissingRegionError
      nil
    end

    def parse_response(response)
      payload = response.body.respond_to?(:read) ? response.body.read : response.body
      envelope = JSON.parse(payload)
      content_text = extract_content_text(envelope)

      JSON.parse(content_text)
    end

    def extract_content_text(envelope)
      if envelope["content"].is_a?(Array)
        text_chunk = envelope["content"].find { |c| c["type"] == "text" }&.fetch("text", nil)
        return text_chunk if text_chunk.present?
      end

      return envelope["completion"] if envelope["completion"].present?

      raise ValidationError, "Bedrock returned an unexpected payload"
    end

    def request_body
      {
        anthropic_version: "bedrock-2023-05-31",
        max_tokens: 256,
        temperature: 0,
        system: system_prompt,
        messages: [
          {
            role: "user",
            content: [
              { type: "text", text: user_prompt }
            ]
          }
        ]
      }
    end

    def system_prompt
      <<~PROMPT.squish
        You are a strict validator for compliance documents. Decode the provided base64 content,
        scan the text, and decide if the document matches the claimed type from the allowed list.
        Only respond with compact JSON: {"verdict":"match|mismatch|unsure","confidence":0-1,"reason":"short explanation"}.
        Choose "match" only when the content clearly aligns with the claimed type; choose "mismatch"
        when it clearly does not; otherwise use "unsure". Do not include any extra text or code fences.
      PROMPT
    end

    def user_prompt
      <<~PROMPT
        Claimed document type: "#{@claimed_type}"
        Allowed document types: #{AccountDocument::DOCUMENT_TYPES.join(", ")}
        Document name: #{@document.document_name.presence || "n/a"}
        File name: #{@document.file&.identifier}
        Base64-encoded file content (may be truncated to #{MAX_BASE64_LENGTH} characters):
        #{truncated_base64}
      PROMPT
    end

    def truncated_base64
      encoded = Base64.strict_encode64(@document.file.read)
      return encoded if encoded.length <= MAX_BASE64_LENGTH

      "#{encoded[0, MAX_BASE64_LENGTH]}... [truncated]"
    end
  end
end

module Api
  module Org
    module V3
      # v3 api endpoints for account_documents
      class AccountDocumentsController < BaseController
        before_action :set_document, only: %i[update destroy validate_type]

        def index
          authorize %i[api org v3 account_document]

          documents = @account.documents.page(page).per(per_page)

          serialize_collection(documents)
        end

        def create
          authorize %i[api org v3 account_document]

          document = @account.documents.new(
            account_documents_params.merge(uploaded_by: current_user)
          )

          if document.save
            serialize_obj(document, status: :created)
          else
            serialize_errors_for(document)
          end
        end

        def update
          authorize [:api, :org, :v3, @document]

          if @document.update(account_documents_params)
            serialize_obj(@document)
          else
            serialize_errors_for(@document)
          end
        end

        def destroy
          authorize [:api, :org, :v3, @document]

          @document.destroy
          head :no_content
        end

        def validate_type
          authorize [:api, :org, :v3, @document], :validate?

          claimed_type = params[:document_type].presence || params[:documentType].presence
          result = AccountDocuments::BedrockValidator.new(
            document: @document,
            claimed_type: claimed_type
          ).call

          render json: {
            id: @document.id,
            documentType: @document.document_type,
            claimedDocumentType: claimed_type || @document.document_type,
            verdict: result.fetch(:verdict),
            confidence: result.fetch(:confidence),
            reason: result.fetch(:reason),
            modelId: result.fetch(:model_id)
          }, status: :ok
        rescue AccountDocuments::BedrockValidator::ConfigurationError => e
          render json: { error: e.message }, status: :unprocessable_entity
        rescue AccountDocuments::BedrockValidator::ValidationError => e
          render json: { error: e.message }, status: :bad_gateway
        end

      private

        def account_documents_params
          params.require(params_wrapper).permit(
            *WRAPPED_MODEL_PARAMS.map(&:underscore)
          ).merge(account_id: @account.id)
        end

        def set_account
          @account = current_account_scope.find_by!(uid: params[:account_id])
        end

        def set_document
          @document = @account.documents.find(params[:id])
        end

        WRAPPED_MODEL_PARAMS = %w[
          accountId
          documentType
          documentName
          file
          id
        ].freeze

        wrap_parameters params_wrapper, include: WRAPPED_MODEL_PARAMS
      end
    end
  end
end

  before { sign_in user }

  path "/org/v3/account_documents" do
    get "Get all Account Documents" do
      tags "Account Documents"
      consumes "application/json"
      produces "application/json"

      parameter name: :account_id,
                in: :query,
                type: :string,
                required: true
      parameter name: :page,
                in: :query,
                type: :integer,
                description: "Page number. Default: 1",
                required: false
      parameter name: :per_page,
                in: :query,
                type: :integer,
                description: "Per page items. Default: 100",
                required: false

      context "when success" do
        let(:account_id) { account.uid }

        before do
          permission_list.access.accounts_write = true
          permission_list.save
        end

        response "200", :ok do
          schema type: :object,
                 properties: {
                   data: {
                     type: :array,
                     items: {
                       "$ref" => "#/components/schemas/account_document"
                     }
                   },
                   meta: {
                     type: :object,
                     properties: {
                       currentPage: { type: :integer },
                       totalPages: { type: :integer },
                       totalCount: { type: :integer }
                     }
                   }
                 }

          run_strict_test! do |response|
            res = JSON.parse(response.body)
            collection = res["data"]

            expect(collection.size).to eq(1)
          end
        end
      end

      context "when failure" do
        context "with incorrect permissions" do
          let(:account_id) { account.uid }

          before do
            permission_list.access.accounts_write = false
            permission_list.save
          end

          response "403", :forbidden do
            run_strict_test!
          end
        end
      end
    end

      tags "Account Documents"
      consumes "application/json"
      produces "application/json"

      parameter name: :params,
                in: :body,
                schema: {
                  type: :object,
                  properties: {
                    accountId: { type: :string },
                    documentType: {
                      type: :string,
                      enum: AccountDocument::DOCUMENT_TYPES
                    },
                    documentName: { type: :string },
                    file: { type: :string }
                  },
                  required: %i[accountId]
                }

      context "when success" do

            documentName: "file.pdf",
            file: "data:application/pdf;base64,#{file}"

        before do

        end

            permission_list.save
          end

          response "403", :forbidden do
            run_strict_test!
          end
        end
      end
    end

module AuthorizationTestHelpers
  module RoleInstrumenation
    def permissions
      public_send(:"permissions_#{id}")
    rescue
      super
    end
  end

  def account_admin(account = nil)
    create(:user).tap do |user|
      account ||= create(:account)
      user.make_access!(account, :admin)
    end
  end

  def account_superadmin(account = nil)
    create(:user).tap do |user|
      account ||= create(:account)
      user.make_access!(account, :superadmin)
    end
  end

  def account_member(account = nil)
    create(:user).tap do |user|
      user.make_access!((account || create(:account)), :standard)
      # assign to an unlocked permission list so permissions can be modified
      permission_list = create(:permission_list, account:)
      user.account_users.find_by(account:).update!(permission_list:)
    end
  end

  def anedot_admin(options = {})
    create(:user, options.merge(email: "admin@anedot.com", anedot_admin_level: "full"))
  end

  def finance_admin(options = {})
    create(:user, options.merge(email: "admin@anedot.com", anedot_admin_level: "finance"))
  end

  def support_admin(options = {})
    create(:user, options.merge(email: "support@anedot.com", anedot_admin_level: "support"))
  end

  def partner_admin(partner = nil, options = {})
    create(:user, options).tap do |user|
      user.make_access!(partner || create(:partner), :admin)
    end
  end
end
